ai_angle_verdict.what_is_ml: Genuine ML/CV lives in Nalaquq's automated-ANALYSIS backend (peer-reviewed JCMC paper): a custom Convolutional Neural Network (deep learning, COCO-pretrained weights) to detect small aluminum-hulled vessels in SAR from consumer-grade UAVs, and a polarized-lens UAV pipeline to track and count migrating salmon. Nalaquq SR page lists 'Automated Salmon Counts'.
ai_angle_verdict.what_is_not: The training teaches HUMAN-piloted drones + FAA Part 107; drones are consumer-grade, hand-launched; NO autonomous flight. Thermal reindeer counting = application/interest only, no evidence it is algorithmic. 'Detect Ancestral Archeological Sites' = project title only, no ML detail; must NOT be framed as AI.
ai_angle_verdict.frame: Alaska-Native pilots trained on drones, with real ML in specific automated-analysis tools (SAR vessel-detection CNN, salmon counting), NOT autonomous AI flight.

claims.text: KYUK published its feature on the Quinhagak drone-training program on July 14, 2026, reported by Samantha Watson.
claims.verbatim_quote: Publication Date: July 14, 2026 ... Reporter: Samantha Watson

claims.text: A drone-training session was held June 9, 2026 in Quinhagak, Alaska.
claims.verbatim_quote: A student of the drone-training course hand-launches a drone into the air. June 9, 2026 in Quinhagak, Alaska.

claims.text: About a dozen trainees from rural Alaska attended the session.
claims.verbatim_quote: a dozen trainees from across rural Alaska learned to fly drones

claims.text: The story aired on Alaska News Nightly on July 14, 2026, reported by Samantha Watson from Bethel.
claims.verbatim_quote: a drone training program takes flight in western Alaska

claims.text: Nalaquq is an Alaska Native-owned subsidiary of Qanirtuuq, Inc., the village corporation for Quinhagak.
claims.verbatim_quote: Nalaquq, LLC, (from the Yugtun Phrase 'It's been found') is an Alaska Native-owned subsidiary of Qanirtuuq, Inc.
claims.note: KYUK calls it the tech subsidiary of Quinhagak's village corporation

claims.text: The program's course is a 14-week commitment that prepares participants for FAA Part 107 drone certification.
claims.verbatim_quote: Are you willing to commit to a 14-week course and to take the exam? ... Prepare participants for the FAA Part 107 certification process.
claims.note: local cohort course, distinct from the June 9 visiting session

claims.text: Applicants must be from Quinhagak, Eek, Goodnews, or Platinum, and a committee selects one individual from each community.
claims.verbatim_quote: Participants must be from Quinhagak, Eek, Goodnews, or Platinum ... A Committee will select 1 individual from each community.

claims.text: The training is funded by an Alaska Department of Transportation grant.
claims.verbatim_quote: Alaska Department of Transportation grant
claims.note: amount not stated; hedge as 'an Alaska DOT grant', no dollar figure

claims.text: Applications of the drones include thermal imaging for reindeer counting, salmon/fish counting on the Kanektok River, and search and rescue.
claims.verbatim_quote: Thermal imaging for reindeer counting ... Fish counting in Kanektok River ... Search and rescue operations
claims.note: reindeer counting NOT claimed as algorithmic

claims.text: Byron Petluska said the program gets weekly search-and-rescue calls, sometimes three times a week, sometimes daily.
claims.verbatim_quote: We get weekly calls for search and rescue -- weekly. Sometimes three times a week. Sometimes every day.

claims.text: Instructor Sean Gleason said local pilots know their traffic patterns better than any outside operator.
claims.verbatim_quote: It's why if you're a local pilot, you're gonna know your traffic patterns better than anyone who's outside
claims.note: attribute to Gleason, not established fact

claims.text: Angelina Olrun, 19, of Mekoryuk, was a trainee interested in reindeer herding.
claims.verbatim_quote: Angelina Olrun, 19 (trainee from Mekoryuk, herding interest)
claims.note: do not claim she performs automated counting

claims.text: The drone program grew out of archaeologists using drones to survey the Nunalleq dig site near Quinhagak.
claims.verbatim_quote: Drones introduced via archaeologists surveying Nunalleq dig site

claims.text: Nalaquq's team built a custom Convolutional Neural Network using deep learning to detect small aluminum-hulled vessels for search and rescue via consumer-grade drones.
claims.verbatim_quote: a custom Convolutional Neural Network that can be deployed over a search grid in real time to detect missing vessels using consumer-grade UAVs
claims.note: BACKGROUND (peer-reviewed JCMC, outside news window); strongest ML evidence; label as prior published work

claims.text: The team used a consumer-grade drone with a polarized lens filter to track and count migrating salmon.
claims.verbatim_quote: a consumer-grade UAV equipped with a polarized lens filter to track and count migrating salmon
claims.note: BACKGROUND grounding for automated salmon counts

claims.text: Nalaquq's services include remote sensing with specialized aircraft and sensors for 2D and 3D mapping of infrastructure and subsistence areas.
claims.verbatim_quote: specialized aircraft and sensors for all your 2D and 3D mapping needs

claims.text: Nalaquq lists 'Automated Salmon Counts' and 'Detect Ancestral Archeological Sites' among its past drone projects.
claims.verbatim_quote: Automated Salmon Counts ... Detect Ancestral Archeological Sites
claims.note: project titles only; 'automated' corroborated for salmon by JCMC; archaeological detection must NOT be framed as AI

honesty_guardrails: The training is HUMAN-piloted drones + FAA Part 107. Never claim autonomous/AI flight.
Real ML = two specific tools: SAR vessel-detection CNN (c14) and salmon counting (c15/c17), sourced to the JCMC paper as Nalaquq's prior published work.
Reindeer counting (c9,c12) and archaeological detection (c13,c17) are NOT AI; frame as drone/thermal applications only.
No DOT dollar figure exists; say 'an Alaska DOT grant'.
The dozen June 9 trainees came from 'across rural Alaska' (unnamed); the 4-community committee selection (c7) is the separate November local cohort. Do not conflate.

claims.text: Alaska DNR proposes conveying ~20,000 acres (~30 sq mi) of state land near Houston (Mat-Su) to AIDEA.
claims.verbatim_quote: 30-square-mile parcel of state land ... just north of the thinly settled city of Houston, in the Mat-Su region

claims.text: The land would be transferred to AIDEA at no charge.
claims.verbatim_quote: Giving the land to AIDEA at no charge, a massive state subsidy

claims.text: The parcel is intended for a multi-use industrial and energy district including scalable data centers and a logistics hub.
claims.verbatim_quote: multi-use industrial and energy development district ... scalable data centers

claims.text: The public comment period runs until August 19, 2026.
claims.verbatim_quote: The department will accept public comment until August 19.

claims.text: DNR (Commissioner John Crowther) is grantor, AIDEA recipient; reported July 13 and July 15, 2026.
claims.verbatim_quote: AIDEA would receive the land from the Department of Natural Resources under Commissioner John Crowther
